```{r pdxmodelptf, cache=TRUE, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
plot(pdx_lgb[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==0], pdx_lgb_A0[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==0], col=colors_for_gg_A[1], xlab="Market pdx", ylab="Portfolio pdx", ylim=c(0,1), xlim=c(0,1), main=long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])
points(pdx_lgb[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==1], pdx_lgb_A1[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==1], col=colors_for_gg_A[2])
})
```


## Unaware premium

```{r unmodel, cache=TRUE}
source('___lgb_unaware.R') 

## clean the preds repo
unlink(file.path('preds', "*_unaware.json"))
unaware_lgb <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(the_unaware_lightgbm_fun)

```

Run unaware for each of the two portfolios: they will differ.

```{r unmodelptf, cache=TRUE}
unaware_lgb_A1 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(the_unaware_lightgbm_fun, selectA=1)
unaware_lgb_A0 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(the_unaware_lightgbm_fun, selectA=0)

par(mfrow=c(2,3))
lapply(names(sims), function(pop_name){
plot(unaware_lgb[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==0], unaware_lgb_A0[[pop_name]]$preds$valid, col=colors_for_gg_A[1], xlab="Market prediction", ylab="Portfolio prediction", ylim=c(80,140), xlim=c(80,140), main=long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])
points(unaware_lgb[[pop_name]]$preds$valid[valid[[pop_name]]$A==1], unaware_lgb_A1[[pop_name]]$preds$valid, col=colors_for_gg_A[2])
})
```


## The OT mapping

Python was set up earlier in the document. 

```{r OTmap}

source_python("___opt_transp.py")

## now, the folder 'transported' exist, with one epsilon_y file per scenario
```


## (Temporary cheat) mapping to corrective

I want a mapping, from (SB, D)  to SC. Optimal transport (previous section) is supposed to be okay, but I ain't convinced yet. 

```{r lgbmapping, cache=TRUE}
source('___lgb_mapping_to_corr.R')

corr_mapping_lgb <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>%
 lapply(the_mapping_to_corr_lightgbm_fun, selectA=0:1)

corr_mapping_lgb_A0 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>%
 lapply(the_mapping_to_corr_lightgbm_fun, selectA=0)

corr_mapping_lgb_A1 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>%
 lapply(the_mapping_to_corr_lightgbm_fun, selectA=1)

```


# The families of premiums

We need a few ggplot2 tools in `___function.R`.

We will store the five families of premium, first we compute them on the entire market.

```{r premcalc}
levels_for_scores <- c("SB", "SU", "SA", "SH", "SC")
labels_for_scores <- c("$S^B(X, D)$","$S^U(X)$", "$S^A(X)$", "$S^H(X)$", "$S^C(\\epsilon_X)$")

scores <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  list("SB" = function(x, d){
    
    ## simple call of the best estimate model
    best_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
  }, "SU" = function(x, d=NULL){
    
    ## simple call of the unaware model
    unaware_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x))
  }, "SA" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## SA = E(SB) unconditional
    sapply(0:1, function(d){best_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))*
       marg_dist[[name]][d + 1]}) %>% apply(1, sum)
   }, "SH" = function(x, d = NULL){

    ## Here we cheated by using our mapping of SC
    ## explicit inference of SC : SH = E(SC|X)
    tab_corr <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      sb <- best_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      corr_mapping_lgb[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = dl))
      })
    tab_pdx <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      pdx_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      })

    (tab_corr * tab_pdx) %>% apply(1, sum)
  }, "SC" = function(x, d){

    ## SC = T^{d \to \star}(SB(x, d))
    sb <- best_lgb[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
    corr_mapping_lgb[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = d))
  }
    )
})

```

We also store the versions by portfolio:

```{r premcalcptf}
levels_for_scores_A <- c("SB", "SU", "SA", "SA.pp", "SH", "SC")
labels_for_scores_A <- c("$S^B(X, D)$","$S^U(X)$", "$S^A(X)$","$S^A_p(X)$", "$S^H(X)$", "$S^C(\\epsilon_X)$")

scores_A0 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  list("SB" = function(x, d){
    
    ## simple call of the best estimate model
    best_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
  }, "SU" = function(x, d=NULL){
    
    ## simple call of the unaware model
    unaware_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x))
  }, "SA" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## SA = E(SB) unconditional
    sapply(0:1, function(d){best_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))*
       marg_dist_A0[[name]][d + 1]}) %>% apply(1, sum)
  },"SA.pp" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## SA = E(SB) unconditional
    sapply(0:1, function(d){best_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))*
       marg_dist[[name]][d + 1]}) %>% apply(1, sum)
  }, "SH" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## Here we cheated by using our mapping of SC
    ## explicit inference of SC : SH = E(SC|X)
    tab_corr <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      sb <- best_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      corr_mapping_lgb_A0[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = dl))
      })
    tab_pdx <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      pdx_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      })

    (tab_corr * tab_pdx) %>% apply(1, sum)
  }, "SC" = function(x, d){

    ## SC = T^{d \to \star}(SB(x, d))
    sb <- best_lgb_A0[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
    corr_mapping_lgb_A0[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = d))
  }
  )
})

scores_A1 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  list("SB" = function(x, d){
    
    ## simple call of the best estimate model
    best_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
  }, "SU" = function(x, d=NULL){
    
    ## simple call of the unaware model
    unaware_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x))
  }, "SA" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## SA = E(SB) unconditional
    sapply(0:1, function(d){best_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))*
       marg_dist_A1[[name]][d + 1]}) %>% apply(1, sum)
  },"SA.pp" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
    ## SA = E(SB) unconditional
    sapply(0:1, function(d){best_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))*
       marg_dist[[name]][d + 1]}) %>% apply(1, sum)
  }, "SH" = function(x, d = NULL){
    
  # Here we cheated by using our mapping of SC
  # explicit inference of SC : SH = E(SC|X)
    tab_corr <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      sb <- best_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      corr_mapping_lgb_A1[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = dl))
      })
    tab_pdx <- sapply(0:1, function(dl){
      pdx_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = dl))
      })

    (tab_corr * tab_pdx) %>% apply(1, sum)
  }, "SC" = function(x, d){

    ## SC = T^{d \to \star}(SB(x, d))
    sb <- best_lgb_A1[[name]]$pred_fun(data.frame(X = x, D = d))
    corr_mapping_lgb_A1[[name]](data.frame(SB = sb, D = d))
  }
  )
})
```


Now let's try. We compute and store each premium on all scenario over a grid of (x,d) values for the plots.

```{r df_to_g}
grid_to_test <- expand.grid(x = seq(-10, 11, by = 0.05),
                            d = c(0, 1))

df_to_g <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  data.frame(grid_to_test, 
           setNames(nm = levels_for_scores) %>% 
  sapply(function(s){
    scores[[name]][[s]](x = grid_to_test$x, d = grid_to_test$d)
  }))
})

df_to_g_A0 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  data.frame(grid_to_test, 
           setNames(nm = levels_for_scores_A) %>% 
  sapply(function(s){
    scores_A0[[name]][[s]](x = grid_to_test$x, d = grid_to_test$d)
  }))
})

df_to_g_A1 <- setNames(nm = names(sims)) %>% lapply(function(name){
  data.frame(grid_to_test, 
           setNames(nm = levels_for_scores_A) %>% 
  sapply(function(s){
    scores_A1[[name]][[s]](x = grid_to_test$x, d = grid_to_test$d)
  }))
})
```

## Best-estimate versus X

```{r BEvsX, fig.width=12, echo=FALSE}

(to_save_BE_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% 
  lapply(function(name){
    ## The premiums
    premDat <- rbind(df_to_g[[name]][,c(1,2,3)],df_to_g_A1[[name]][,c(1,2,3)],df_to_g_A0[[name]][,c(1,2,3)]) %>% mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1]))
    
    ## the colors
    premDat$Portfolio <- factor(premDat$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(premDat$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Best-estimate premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Best-estimate premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    }
    
    ## lets graph
    premDat %>% 
      mutate(the_population = name) %>% 
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x = x, y = SB,
             col = Portfolio,
             fill = Portfolio,
             lty = Portfolio,
             linewidth = factor(d))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 2), name = "A") +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(1.7, 0.85, 1), name = 'd') + 
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(1, 0.3), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_id])) +
       # title = latex2exp::TeX("$D \\nrightarrow X $")) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  + # see above
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) + 
  theme_classic() + 
#  the_y_scale +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5)),  # Adjust linewidth for 'Pop.' legend
         fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5)),   # Ensure consistency across color and fill
         linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5))) +  # Adjust linetype legend if needed
  
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
  }) %>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_BE_vsX.pdf",
       plot = to_save_BE_vsX,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```

## Unaware vs X

```{r UnawarevsX, fig.width=12, echo=FALSE}
(to_save_unaware_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
graph_to_return <- rbind(df_to_g[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,4)],df_to_g_A1[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,4)],df_to_g_A0[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,4)]) %>% mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1]))

## the colors
    graph_to_return$Portfolio <- factor(graph_to_return$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(graph_to_return$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Unaware premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Unaware premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    }
    
    
graph_to_return %>%
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=x, y=SU,
             col=Portfolio,
             fill=Portfolio,
             linetype =Portfolio))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 2), name = "A") +
labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name]))+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) +
    theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_unaware_vsX.pdf",
       plot = to_save_unaware_vsX,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```


## Aware vs X

The first graph is the aware by portfolio. The second graph is the "unbiased" version of the aware premium, computed with the marginal distribution of D in the population.

```{r AwarevsX, fig.width=12, echo=FALSE}

(to_save_Aware_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
graph_to_return <- rbind(df_to_g[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,5)],df_to_g_A1[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,5)],df_to_g_A0[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,5)]) %>% mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1])) 

## the colors
    graph_to_return$Portfolio <- factor(graph_to_return$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(graph_to_return$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    }
    
  graph_to_return %>%
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=x, y=SA, 
             col=Portfolio,
             fill=Portfolio,
             linetype =Portfolio))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 2), name = "A") +
labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name]))+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) +
    theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
  })%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_Aware_vsX.pdf",
       plot = to_save_Aware_vsX,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

(to_save_Awarepop_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
graph_to_return <- df_to_g[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,5)]%>% rename(SA.pp = SA) %>%
  rbind(df_to_g_A1[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,6)],df_to_g_A0[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,6)])%>% 
  mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1])) 

## the colors
    graph_to_return$Portfolio <- factor(graph_to_return$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(graph_to_return$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium (market prop)")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium (market prop)")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    }

graph_to_return %>%
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=x, y=SA.pp, 
             col=Portfolio,
             fill=Portfolio,
             linetype =Portfolio))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 2), name = "A") +
labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name]))+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) +
    theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_AwarePop_vsX.pdf",
       plot = to_save_Awarepop_vsX,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```


## Corrective

```{r CorrectivevsX, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12}
(to_save_Corrective_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
graph_to_return <- rbind(df_to_g[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,7)],df_to_g_A1[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,8)],df_to_g_A0[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,8)]) %>% mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1])) 
    
## the colors
    graph_to_return$Portfolio <- factor(graph_to_return$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(graph_to_return$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Corrective premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Corrective premium")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    }
    
  graph_to_return %>%
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
 ggplot(aes(x = x, y = SC,
             col = Portfolio,
             fill = Portfolio,
             lty = Portfolio,
             linewidth = factor(d))) +
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 2), name = "A") +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(1.7, 0.85, 1), name = 'd') + 
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(1, 0.3), guide = "none") +
  labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_id])) +
       # title = latex2exp::TeX("$D \\nrightarrow X $")) + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  + # see above
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) + 
  theme_classic() + 
#  the_y_scale +
  guides(color = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5)),  # Adjust linewidth for 'Pop.' legend
         fill = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5)),   # Ensure consistency across color and fill
         linetype = guide_legend(override.aes = list(linewidth = 1.5))) +  # Adjust linetype legend if needed
  
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
  })%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_Corrective_vsX.pdf",
       plot = to_save_Corrective_vsX,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```


## Mean premium paid over market

The theoretical versions of these graphs are easier to grasp, so we might not keep this one?

```{r}
colors_for_gg_premium2 <- c("Best"=colors_for_gg_premium[1],
                           "Unaware"=colors_for_gg_premium[2],
                           "Aware"= colors_for_gg_premium[3])

(to_save_meanprem <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){

premB_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]


premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premU_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premU_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SU(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premU_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SU(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

xseq <- seq(-10.5,12, by=0.5)
Xdiscretized <- cut(test[[pop_name]]$X, xseq)
midpoint.vec <- xseq[-length(xseq)] + diff(xseq)/2
names(midpoint.vec) <- levels(Xdiscretized)
Xmid <- midpoint.vec[Xdiscretized]
  
  pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(1,4)){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Premium")
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous()
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous(labels = NULL)
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      }
  
aggregate(cbind(Best, Unaware, Aware)~Xdisc+D, FUN=mean,
          data=test[[pop_name]]%>% mutate(Aware = premA_bycomp, 
                                          Best = premB_bycomp, 
                                          Unaware = premU_bycomp,
                                          Corrective = premC_bycomp,
                                          Xdisc = Xmid))%>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("Best", "Unaware", "Aware"))%>%
    filter(Xdisc >= -8, Xdisc <= 10) %>%
ggplot(aes(x=Xdisc, y=value,
           col=variable,
           lty=factor(D),
           alpha=factor(D),
           linewidth=factor(D)))+
  geom_line(alpha=0.75)+ 
  labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_id])) + 
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-10,10,5))  + # see above
     # the_y_scale +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))+
 # scale_color_manual(values = c(rev(gray.colors(4))[c(2,4)],1), name = "Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = unname(colors_for_gg_premium2), name = "Premium") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 2), name = "d") +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(0.75, 1), name = "d") +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(1, 0.55), name = 'd') 

  })%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_mean_prem_paid.pdf",
       plot = to_save_meanprem,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

```{r meanpremcor}

(to_save_meanprem_cor <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){

premB_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

xseq <- seq(-10.5,12, by=0.5)
Xdiscretized <- cut(test[[pop_name]]$X, xseq)
midpoint.vec <- xseq[-length(xseq)] + diff(xseq)/2
names(midpoint.vec) <- levels(Xdiscretized)
Xmid <- midpoint.vec[Xdiscretized]
  
  pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(1,4)){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Premium")
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous()
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous(labels = NULL)
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      }
  
aggregate(cbind(Best, Corrective)~Xdisc+D, FUN=mean,
          data=test[[pop_name]]%>% mutate(Best = premB_bycomp, 
                                          Corrective = premC_bycomp,
                                          Xdisc = Xmid))%>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("Best", "Corrective"))%>%
    filter(Xdisc >= -8, Xdisc <= 10) %>%
ggplot(aes(x=Xdisc, y=value,
           col=variable,
           lty=factor(D),
           alpha=factor(D),
           linewidth=factor(D)))+
  geom_line(alpha=0.75)+ 
  labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_id])) + 
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-10,10,5))  + # see above
     # the_y_scale +
  theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))+
  scale_color_manual(values = colorspace::darken(unname(colors_for_gg_premium[c(1, 4)]), 0.2), name = "Premium") +
 # scale_color_manual(values = unname(colors_for_gg_premium2), name = "Premium") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 2), name = "d") +
  scale_alpha_manual(values = c(0.75, 1), name = "d") +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(1, 0.55), name = 'd') 

  })%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_mean_prem_paid_cor.pdf",
       plot = to_save_meanprem_cor,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

Version in French for a presentation (not used).

```{r}
to_save_meanprem_list_forpres <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){

premB_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premB_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premA_market <- scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)

xseq <- seq(-10.5,12, by=0.5)
Xdiscretized <- cut(test[[pop_name]]$X, xseq)
midpoint.vec <- xseq[-length(xseq)] + diff(xseq)/2
names(midpoint.vec) <- levels(Xdiscretized)
Xmid <- midpoint.vec[Xdiscretized]
  
  pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(1,4)){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Valeur de la prime")
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous()
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_y_scale <- scale_y_continuous(labels = NULL)
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("x")
      }
  
aggregate(cbind(Best, Aware, Aware_market)~Xdisc+D, FUN=mean,
          data=test[[pop_name]]%>% mutate(Aware = premA_bycomp, 
                                          Aware_market = premA_market,
                                          Best = premB_bycomp, 
                                          Xdisc = Xmid))%>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("Best", "Aware", "Aware_market"))%>%
    filter(Xdisc >= -8, Xdisc <= 10) %>%
ggplot(aes(x=Xdisc, y=value,
           col=variable,
           lty=factor(D),
           linewidth=factor(D)))+
  geom_line(alpha=0.75)+ 
  labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_id])) + 
      scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-10,10,5))  + # see above
     # the_y_scale +
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))+
#  scale_color_manual(values = c(rev(gray.colors(4))[c(2,4)],1), name = "Type") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c(1, 2, gray.colors(3)[2]), name = "Prime", 
                     labels=c("Précise", "Consciente par ass.", "Consciente complet")) +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 2), name = "D") +
  scale_linewidth_manual(values = c(1, 0.8), name = 'd') 

  })

(to_save_meanprem_for_pres <- to_save_meanprem_list_forpres %>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 12, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

to_save_meanprem_list_forpres[[1]]

```

# Fairness Criteria

In this section, we assess whether the fairness criteria are verified.

## Big fairness table 

```{r}

the_raw_content_for_big_table <- setNames(nm = names(test)[1:3]) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  setNames(nm = c('1', 'full')) %>%  lapply(function(pop_A_ref){
    setNames(nm = c('1', '0', 'unbiased', 'mixture')) %>%  lapply(function(pop_A_train){
          
      
          full_local_test <- test[[pop_name]]
          local_test_A1 <- full_local_test[full_local_test$A == 1, ]
          
          if(pop_A_ref == '1'){
            local_test <- local_test_A1
          } else {
            local_test <- full_local_test
          }
          
          ## for balancing
          mean_Y_loc <- mean(local_test$Y)
          mean_Y_full <- mean(full_local_test$Y)
          
          if(pop_A_train == '1'){
            scores_to_compute <- scores_A1[[pop_name]] 
            } else if(pop_A_train == '0'){
              scores_to_compute <- scores_A0[[pop_name]] 
            } else {
            scores_to_compute <- scores[[pop_name]]
          }
          
          the_preds_table_local <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC', 'SA_unb')) %>% sapply(function(the_score){
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == 'full') return(scores[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = local_test$X, d = local_test$D))
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == '1') return(scores_A1[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = local_test$X, d = local_test$D))
            scores_to_compute[[the_score]](x = local_test$X,
                                           d = local_test$D)
          }) %>% as.data.frame()
          
          the_preds_table_local_unb <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC', 'SA_unb')) %>% sapply(function(the_score){
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == 'full') return(scores[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = local_test$X, d = local_test$D))
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == '1') return(scores_A1[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = local_test$X, d = local_test$D))
            scores_to_compute[[the_score]](x = local_test$X,
                                           d = local_test$D)
          }) %>% as.data.frame()
          
          factor_balance <- mean_Y_loc/apply(the_preds_table_local, 2, mean)
          factor_balance_unb <- mean_Y_full/apply(the_preds_table_local_unb, 2, mean)
          
          the_preds_table_full <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC', 'SA_unb')) %>% sapply(function(the_score){
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == 'full') return(scores[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = full_local_test$X, d = full_local_test$D))
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == '1') return(scores_A1[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = full_local_test$X, d = full_local_test$D))
            scores_to_compute[[the_score]](x = full_local_test$X,
                                           d = full_local_test$D)
          }) %>% as.data.frame()
          
          the_preds_table_full_unb <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC', 'SA_unb')) %>% sapply(function(the_score){
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == 'full') return(scores[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = full_local_test$X, d = full_local_test$D))
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb' & pop_A_ref == '1') return(scores_A1[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = full_local_test$X, d = full_local_test$D))
            scores[[pop_name]][[the_score]](x = full_local_test$X,
                                           d = full_local_test$D)
          }) %>% as.data.frame()
          
          
          the_preds_table_mixture <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC', 'SA_unb')) %>% sapply(function(the_score){
            if(the_score == 'SA_unb') return(scores[[pop_name]][['SA']](x = full_local_test$X, d = full_local_test$D))
              
            prem_A1 <- scores_A1[[pop_name]][[the_score]](x = full_local_test$X,
                                                  d = full_local_test$D) * factor_balance[the_score]
            prem_A0 <- scores_A0[[pop_name]][[the_score]](x = full_local_test$X,
                                                  d = full_local_test$D) * factor_balance[the_score]
            1:nrow(full_local_test) %>% sapply(function(i){
              if(full_local_test$A[i] == 1){
                return(prem_A1[i])
              } else if(full_local_test$A[i] == 0){
                return(prem_A0[i])
              }
            })
            }) %>% as.data.frame()
          
        return(
          list('loc_data' = local_test, 
               'full_data' = full_local_test, 
               'loc_table' = sweep(the_preds_table_local, 2, factor_balance, '*'),
               'loc_table_unb' = sweep(the_preds_table_local, 2, factor_balance_unb, '*'),
               'full_table' =  sweep(the_preds_table_full, 2, factor_balance, '*'),
               'full_table_unb' = sweep(the_preds_table_full, 2, factor_balance_unb, '*'), 
               'mixture_table' =  sweep(the_preds_table_mixture, 2, factor_balance_unb, '*'))
        )  
    })
  })
})

the_tables <- setNames(nm = names(test)[1:3]) %>%  lapply(function(scenario){
  
to_fill <- data.frame('i' = rep(1:6, 3),
                      'scenario' = rep(scenario, (2 + 4)*3),
                      'qty' = c(rep('LR', 6), rep('Proxy', 6), rep('DP', 6)),
           'pop_ref' = rep(c(rep('1', 2), rep('full', 4)), 3),
           'mod_type' = rep(c('1', 'unbiased', '1',
                              '0', 'unbiased', 'mixture'), 3))

to_fill$SB <- rep(NA, nrow(to_fill))
to_fill$SA <- rep(NA, nrow(to_fill))
to_fill$SC <- rep(NA, nrow(to_fill))

wasserstein_by_group <- function(values, group) {
  # Check inputs
  if (length(values) != length(group)) stop("Length mismatch between values and group.")
  if (length(unique(group)) != 2) stop("Group must be binary.")
  
  # Convert group to factor with two levels
  group <- as.factor(group)
  
  # Split values by group
  values0 <- values[group == levels(group)[1]]
  values1 <- values[group == levels(group)[2]]
  
  # Load transport package for wasserstein1d
  if (!requireNamespace("transport", quietly = TRUE)) {
    install.packages("transport")
  }
  library(transport)
  
  # Compute 1D Wasserstein distance
  return(wasserstein1d(values0, values1))
}

best_shift_mse_optimize <- function(x, y) {
  if (length(x) != length(y)) stop("x and y must have the same length")
  
  # Define the MSE function to minimize
  mse_function <- function(c) {
    mean((x + c - y)^2)
  }
  
  # Optimize over a reasonable range (e.g., -1000 to 1000)
  result <- optimize(mse_function, interval = c(-1e3, 1e3))
  
  # Return both optimal constant and minimum MSE
  return(result$minimum)
}


for (i in unique(to_fill$i)) {
  scenario <- to_fill[i, 'scenario']
  ref_pop <- to_fill[i, 'pop_ref']
  model <- to_fill[i, 'mod_type']
  
  if(ref_pop == '1'){
    pop_eval_loc <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$loc_data
    if(model == 'unbiased'){
      the_preds_table <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$loc_table
    } else {
      the_preds_table <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$loc_table_unb
    }
  } else {
    pop_eval_loc <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$full_data
    if(model  == 'mixture'){
      the_preds_table <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$mixture_table
    } else if(model  == '1'){
      the_preds_table <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$full_table
    } else {
      the_preds_table <- the_raw_content_for_big_table[[scenario]][[ref_pop]][[model]]$full_table_unb
    }
  }
  
  
  table_to_return <- setNames(nm = c('SB', 'SA', 'SC')) %>% sapply(function(score){
    ## Loss ratio parity
    LR <- pop_eval_loc$Y/the_preds_table[[score]]
    LR_par <- wasserstein_by_group(LR, group = pop_eval_loc$D)
    
    ## Demographic parity
    demo_par <- wasserstein_by_group(the_preds_table[[score]], group = pop_eval_loc$D)
    
    ## PD metric... 
    const_opt <- best_shift_mse_optimize(the_preds_table[[score]], the_preds_table[['SA_unb']])
    proxy <- wasserstein_by_group(the_preds_table[[score]] + const_opt - the_preds_table[['SA_unb']], 
                         group = pop_eval_loc$D)
    
    names_to_output <- c('LR', 'Proxy', 'DP')
    setNames(c(LR_par, proxy, demo_par),
             nm = names_to_output)
  })
  
  to_fill[to_fill$i == i, c('SB', 'SA', 'SC')] <- table_to_return
}

return(to_fill)

})


the_tables %>% do.call(rbind, .) %>% 
  filter(!(pop_ref == 'full' & mod_type == '0'),
         !(pop_ref == '1' & mod_type == 'unbiased')) %>% 
  xtable::xtable(digits = 3)
```

```{r}
saveRDS(the_tables %>% do.call(rbind, .), 'preds/metrics_tables')
```


## Aware premium

### Premium Independence criterion

```{r AwareIndep, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}


# 
# 
# (to_save_SA_indep <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
# premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
# premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
# premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]
# 
# melted_version <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premA_bycomp = premA_bycomp,premA = scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)) %>%
#   reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("premA_bycomp", "premA")) %>% 
#     mutate(variable = factor(variable,
#                              levels = c("premA_bycomp", "premA"),
#                              labels = c("Portfolio-specific", "Market")))%>%
#   mutate(premAware = value)
# 
# ggplot(melted_version, aes(x=premAware, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
#   geom_density(alpha=0.5)+
#   facet_grid(~variable)+
#   theme_classic()+
#   scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
#   scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
#   ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
#   labs(x="Aware premium", fill="D", col="D")
# 
# }))

```


### Loss Ratio Independence

It is seen that the aware by company is closer to actuarial fairness than when using the complete market:

```{r, fig.height=10, echo=FALSE}
(to_save_LR_indep <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  
premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

melted_version <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premA = scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X,
                                                                            d=test[[pop_name]]$D))%>%
  mutate(LRA = Y/premA, LRA_bycomp = Y/premA_bycomp)  %>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("LRA_bycomp", "LRA")) %>% 
    mutate(variable = factor(variable,
                             levels = c("LRA_bycomp", "LRA"),
                             labels = c("Portfolio-specific", "Market")))%>%
  mutate(LossRatio = value)

ggplot(melted_version, aes(x=LossRatio, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.5)+
  facet_grid(~variable)+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
  scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
  ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
  labs(x="Loss ratio", fill="D", col="D") + 
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.7, 1.3),
                     breaks = c(0.8, 1, 1.2)) 

})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))


ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_LRAware.pdf",
       plot = to_save_LR_indep,
       height = 7,
       width = 11,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

### Separation criterion

Here it is also visible:

```{r, fig.width=12}
vec_cut <- quantile(valid$original$Y, seq(0,1,0.1)) +c(-0.5, rep(0,9),0.5)


(tosave_Aware_givenDY <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
groupy <- cut(test[[pop_name]]$Y, vec_cut)

premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

melted_version <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premA_bycomp = premA_bycomp, groupy=groupy, premA = scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)) %>%
  filter(Y>vec_cut[3],Y<=vec_cut[9]) %>% droplevels() %>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("premA_bycomp", "premA")) %>% 
    mutate(variable = factor(variable,
                             levels = c("premA_bycomp", "premA"),
                             labels = c("Portfolio-specific", "Market")))%>%
  mutate(premAware=value)


ggplot(melted_version, aes(x=premAware, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.5)+theme_bw()+facet_grid(groupy~variable)+
  theme_minimal()+
    scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
    scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
  ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])
})%>%
ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           ncol = 6, nrow=1,
                           widths = 23, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

```

## Corrective Premium fairness criteria

### Premium independence criterion

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}

(to_save_SC_indep <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)

v0 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==0, ]
v1 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==1, ]
v <- valid[[pop_name]]

pb_0 <- sum(v0$Y) / sum(scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v0$X, d=v0$D))
pb_1 <- sum(v1$Y) / sum(scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v1$X, d=v1$D))
mb <- sum(v0$Y) / sum(scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v$X, d=v$D))

premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] * pb_0
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] * pb_1
premC <- scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D) * mb


melted_version <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premC_bycomp = premC_bycomp,premC = scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)) %>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("premC_bycomp", "premC")) %>% 
    mutate(variable = factor(variable,
                             levels = c("premC_bycomp", "premC"),
                             labels = c("Portfolio-specific", "Market")))%>%
  mutate(premCorrective = value)

ggplot(melted_version, aes(x=premCorrective, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.5)+
  facet_grid(~variable)+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(80, 140),
                     breaks = c(90, 110, 130)) + 
  scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
    scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
  ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
  labs(x="Corrective premium", fill="D", col="D")

})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_Corrective.pdf",
       plot = to_save_SC_indep,
       height = 7,
       width = 11,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}

to_save_SC_indep2 <- function(pop_name){
premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)

v0 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==0, ]
v1 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==1, ]
v <- valid[[pop_name]]

pb_0 <- sum(v0$Y) / sum(scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v0$X, d=v0$D))
pb_1 <- sum(v1$Y) / sum(scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v1$X, d=v1$D))
mb <- sum(v$Y) / sum(scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v$X, d=v$D))

premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] * pb_0
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] * pb_1
premC <- scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D) * mb


df_to_g_bycomp <- data.frame(muCA = premC_bycomp, 
                      muC = premC,
                      'D' = test[[pop_name]]$D,
                      'A' = c(test[[pop_name]]$A))

df_to_g_market <- df_to_g_bycomp %>% mutate(A = 2)


appender <- function(string) latex2exp::TeX(string) 

rbind(df_to_g_bycomp, df_to_g_market) %>% 
  mutate(A = factor(A, levels = c(0, 2, 1),
                    labels = c('$A=0$', 'Market', '$A=1$'))) %>% 
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c('muCA', 'muC')) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = c('muCA', 'muC'),
                           labels = c('$S = \\mu^C_{pb}(X, d, A)$',
                                                            '$S = \\mu^C_{mb}(X, d)$')
                           )) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x=value, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D), lty = factor(D), size = factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.4, bw = 1.5)+
  facet_grid(cols = vars(A), rows = vars(variable),
             labeller = as_labeller(appender, default = label_parsed))+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(80, 140),
                     breaks = c(90, 110, 130)) + 
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D, name = 'd')+
    scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D, name = 'd')+
  scale_linetype_manual(values=c('solid', '33'), name = 'd')+
  scale_size_manual(values=c(1, 0.7), name = 'd')+
  #ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
  labs(x="s",
       y = latex2exp::TeX('$\\hat{f}_{S|D, A}(s|d, a)$'),
       fill="D", col="D") 
}

to_save_SC_indep2('original')

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_Corrective2.pdf",
       plot = to_save_SC_indep2('original'),
       height = 3.25,
       width = 8,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

```{r, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}

(to_save_SC_indep_full <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)

v0 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==0,]
v1 <- valid[[pop_name]][valid[[pop_name]]$A==1,]
v <- valid[[pop_name]]

pb_0 <- sum(v0$Y) / sum(scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v0$X, d=v0$D))
pb_1 <- sum(v1$Y) / sum(scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v1$X, d=v1$D))
mb <- sum(v$Y) / sum(scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = v$X, d=v$D))

premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] * pb_0
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] * pb_1
premC <- scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D) * mb

df_to_g_bycomp <- data.frame(muCA = premC_bycomp, 
                      muC = premC,
                      'D' = test[[pop_name]]$D,
                      'A' = c(test[[pop_name]]$A))

df_to_g_market <- df_to_g_bycomp %>% mutate(A = 2)


appender <- function(string) latex2exp::TeX(string) 

rbind(df_to_g_bycomp, df_to_g_market) %>% 
  mutate(A = factor(A, levels = c(0, 2, 1),
                    labels = c('$A=0$', 'Market', '$A=1$'))) %>% 
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c('muCA', 'muC')) %>%
  mutate(variable = factor(variable, levels = c('muCA', 'muC'),
                           labels = c('$S = \\mu^C_{pb}(X, d|A)$',
                                                            '$S = \\mu^C_{mb}(X, d)$')
                           )) %>% 
ggplot(aes(x=value, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.5, bw = 2)+
  facet_grid(cols = vars(A), rows = vars(variable),
             labeller = as_labeller(appender, default = label_parsed),
             scales = "free_x", space = "free_x")+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D, name = 'd')+
    scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D, name = 'd')+
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = c(0, 0.02, 0.04)) + 
  ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
  labs(x="s",
       y = latex2exp::TeX('$\\hat{f}_{S|D, A}(s|d, a)$'),
       fill="D", col="D") + 
  theme(strip.text.y = element_text(size = 6),
        axis.text.x = element_text(size = 6))
})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))

ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_Corrective_full.pdf",
       plot = to_save_SC_indep_full,
       height = 7,
       width = 11,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```

### Loss Ratio Independence

Closer to actuarial fairness than when using the complete market:

```{r, fig.height=10, echo=FALSE}
(to_save_LRC_indep <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  
premC_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premC_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

melted_version <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premC = scores[[pop_name]]$SC(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D))%>%
  mutate(LRC = Y/premC, LRC_bycomp = Y/premC_bycomp)  %>%
  reshape2::melt(measure.vars = c("LRC_bycomp", "LRC")) %>% 
    mutate(variable = factor(variable,
                             levels = c("LRC_bycomp", "LRC"),
                             labels = c("Portfolio-specific", "Market")))%>%
  mutate(LossRatio = value)

ggplot(melted_version, aes(x=LossRatio, fill=factor(D), col=factor(D)))+
  geom_density(alpha=0.5)+
  facet_grid(~variable)+
  theme_classic()+
  scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
    scale_fill_manual(values=colors_for_gg_D)+
    scale_linetype_manual(values=1:2, name = 'd')+
  ggtitle(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])+
  labs(x="Loss ratio", fill="D", col="D")+
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(0.65, 1.35),
                     breaks = c(0.8, 1, 1.2)) 

})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))


ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_LRCorrective.pdf",
       plot = to_save_LRC_indep,
       height = 7,
       width = 11,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)

```




# Results on test set

Here we try to see the aggregate LR and Average premiums by group. We do not keep this representation in the end.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=9}

pop_name <- "original"

testLRandprem <- lapply(names(sims) ,function(pop_name) {
  test_premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_premApp_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premApp_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premApp_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_premU_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premU_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SU(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premU_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SU(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_premBE_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premBE_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premBE_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SB(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_wprem <- test[[pop_name]] %>% mutate(premA = test_premA_bycomp, 
                            premA.pp = test_premApp_bycomp, 
                            premU = test_premU_bycomp,
                            premBE = test_premBE_bycomp,
                            premAmark = scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X,
                                                               d=test[[pop_name]]$D))

LossRatios <- test_wprem %>% 
  aggregate(cbind(premBE, premU, premA, premA.pp, premAmark, Y)~D, FUN=sum) %>%
  mutate(LRBE = Y/premBE, LRU = Y/premU, LRA = Y/premA, 
         LRpp = Y/premA.pp, LRmarket = Y/premAmark)%>%
  select(D, LRBE, LRU, LRA, LRpp, LRmarket)%>%
  rename(BE=LRBE, Unaware=LRU, Aware.bycomp = LRA, Aware.pp = LRpp, Aware.market = LRmarket)%>%
reshape2::melt(id.vars = 1, measure.vars = 2:6, variable.name = "family", value.name="LR")

# test_wprem %>%
# aggregate(cbind(premBE, premU, premA, premA.pp, premAmark, Y)~D+A, FUN=sum) %>%
#   mutate(LRBE = Y/premBE, LRU = Y/premU, LRA = Y/premA, 
#          LRpp = Y/premA.pp, LRmarket = Y/premAmark)

#test_wprem %>%
#  aggregate(cbind(premBE, premU, premA, premA.pp, premAmark)~D+A, FUN=mean)

AvgPrem <- test_wprem %>%
  aggregate(cbind(premBE, premU, premA, premA.pp, premAmark)~D, FUN=mean)%>%
  rename(BE=premBE, Unaware=premU, Aware.bycomp = premA, Aware.pp = premA.pp, Aware.market = premAmark)%>%
reshape2::melt(id.vars = 1, measure.vars = 2:6, variable.name = "family", value.name="AvgPrem")

merge(AvgPrem, LossRatios)%>%
  reshape2::melt(id.vars=1:2, measure.vars=3:4)})

names(testLRandprem) <- names(sims)


listofplotLRPREM <- names(testLRandprem) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){ 
  ggplot(testLRandprem[[pop_name]], aes(x=family, fill=factor(D), y = value))+
  geom_bar(stat="identity", position=position_dodge())+
  facet_wrap(~variable, scales="free_y")+
  theme_minimal()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle=45, hjust=1))+
  labs(title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])) })

ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist=listofplotLRPREM,
                          ncol = 3, nrow=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right')
```


## other graphs


```{r}
names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
pdx_test_byComp <- rep(0, n/10)
pdx_test_byComp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-
  pdx_lgb_A0[[pop_name]]$preds$test[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]
pdx_test_byComp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-
  pdx_lgb_A1[[pop_name]]$preds$test[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]

test[[pop_name]]%>% mutate(pdx = pdx_test_byComp, D= factor(D)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x=X, colour = D, y = pdx))+
  geom_point(alpha=0.5)+
  theme_minimal()+
   labs(x = latex2exp::TeX("$x$"),
       y = latex2exp::TeX("$P(D=1|X=x,A)$"),
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])) }
)%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right')

names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
pdmarg_test_byComp <- rep(0, n/10)
pdmarg_test_byComp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-
  marg_dist_A0[[pop_name]][2]
pdmarg_test_byComp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-
  marg_dist_A1[[pop_name]][2]

test[[pop_name]]%>% mutate(pdmarg = pdmarg_test_byComp, D= factor(D)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(y=X, x = factor(pdmarg)))+
  geom_boxplot()+
  theme_minimal()+
   labs(y = latex2exp::TeX("$x$"),
       x = latex2exp::TeX("$P(D=1|A)$"),
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name])) }
)%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 3, ncol=2,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right')
```

```{r}

```


# Premium rebalancing

I want to compare the SA, the SA.pp and the SA.pp rebalanced.

```{r}

sss <- scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(sims[[pop_name]]$X, sims[[pop_name]]$D)[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

sss <- sss*sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0])/sum(sss)


SA_rebal_ratios <- names(df_to_g_A1) %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  
premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n)
premA_bycomp[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = sims[[pop_name]]$X, d=sims[[pop_name]]$D)[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premA_bycomp[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = sims[[pop_name]]$X, d=sims[[pop_name]]$D)[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

premA <-  scores[[pop_name]]$SA(x = sims[[pop_name]]$X, d=sims[[pop_name]]$D)

premApp_bycomp <- rep(0, n)
premApp_bycomp[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = sims[[pop_name]]$X, d=sims[[pop_name]]$D)[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
premApp_bycomp[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = sims[[pop_name]]$X, d=sims[[pop_name]]$D)[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

ratios <- c(sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y)/sum(premA), sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0])/sum(premA_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0]),
  sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1])/sum(premA_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1]),
  sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0])/sum(premApp_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0]),
    sum(sims[[pop_name]]$Y[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1])/sum(premApp_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1]))

names(ratios) <- c("market", "A0", "A1", "App0", "App1")

premA_rebal <- premA*ratios[1]
  
premA_bycomp_rebal <- rep(0, n)
premA_bycomp_rebal[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <- premA_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1]*ratios[3]
premA_bycomp_rebal[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <- premA_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0]*ratios[2]


premApp_bycomp_rebal <- rep(0, n)
premApp_bycomp_rebal[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <- premApp_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==1]*ratios[5]
premApp_bycomp_rebal[which(sims[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <- premApp_bycomp[sims[[pop_name]]$A==0]*ratios[4]


## on test data to reduce size of graph here
test_premA_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premA_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_premApp_bycomp <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premApp_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <-  scores_A1[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)]
test_premApp_bycomp[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <-  scores_A0[[pop_name]]$SA.pp(x = test[[pop_name]]$X, d=test[[pop_name]]$D)[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)]

test_premA_bycomp_rebal <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premA_bycomp_rebal[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <- test_premA_bycomp[test[[pop_name]]$A==1]*ratios[3]
test_premA_bycomp_rebal[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <- test_premA_bycomp[test[[pop_name]]$A==0]*ratios[2]


test_premApp_bycomp_rebal <- rep(0, n/10)
test_premApp_bycomp_rebal[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==1)] <- test_premApp_bycomp[test[[pop_name]]$A==1]*ratios[5]
test_premApp_bycomp_rebal[which(test[[pop_name]]$A==0)] <- test_premApp_bycomp[test[[pop_name]]$A==0]*ratios[4]

##
list(ratios=ratios, premApp_bycomp_rebal=premApp_bycomp_rebal, premA_bycomp_rebal=premA_bycomp_rebal, premA_rebal=premA_rebal,
     test_premApp_bycomp_rebal=test_premApp_bycomp_rebal,
     test_premA_bycomp_rebal=test_premA_bycomp_rebal)
})
names(SA_rebal_ratios) <- names(sims)

lapply(names(sims), function(i) SA_rebal_ratios[[i]]$ratios)

```


With the pp versions, we seem to have problems with scenarios 1 to 3. We see in the following graph that we find again a similar pattern as with the "Portfolio-specific" version of the Aware score, after we rebalance.

```{r}
(to_save_Awarepoprebal_vsX <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  
  temp <- df_to_g[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,5)]
  temp$SA <- temp$SA*SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$ratios["market"]
  
  temp_1 <- df_to_g_A1[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,6)]
  temp_1$SA.pp <- temp_1$SA.pp*SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$ratios["App1"]
  temp_0 <- df_to_g_A0[[pop_name]][,c(1,2,6)]
  temp_0$SA.pp <- temp_0$SA.pp*SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$ratios["App0"]
  
graph_to_return <- temp %>% rename(SA.pp = SA) %>%
  rbind(temp_1, temp_0)%>% 
  mutate(Portfolio=rep(c("market","A=1","A=0"), each=dim(df_to_g_A0$original)[1])) 


## the colors
    graph_to_return$Portfolio <- factor(graph_to_return$Portfolio,
                                levels = names(colors_for_gg_market))
    
    ## Make sure to keep the right order
    cols <- levels(graph_to_return$Portfolio) %>% sapply(function(lvl){
      colors_for_gg_market[which(lvl == names(colors_for_gg_market))] %>% unname
    })
    
    pop_id <- which(names(df_to_g_A1) == pop_name)
    
    # keep only axis for last plot
    if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium (market prop + rebalanced)")
      the_x_label <- NULL
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("Aware premium (market prop + rebalanced)")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$x$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- NULL
    }

graph_to_return %>%
  filter(x >= -9, x <= 10) %>% 
  ggplot(aes(x=x, y=SA.pp, 
             col=Portfolio,
             fill=Portfolio,
             linetype =Portfolio))+
  geom_line(alpha = 0.75, linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = cols, name = "A") +
  scale_linetype_manual(values = c(1, 3, 5), name = "A") +
labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name]))+
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = c(-3:3)*4 )  +
    scale_y_continuous(breaks = seq(70, 150, by=20), limits=c(70,150)) +
    theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))
})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))
```


Compare the premiums after rebalancing: Aware versus Aware.pp:

```{r rebal1, fig.width=12, fig.height=8, echo=FALSE}
(to_save_rebal <- names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% lapply(function(pop_name){
  
  df_to_graph <- data.frame(Aware = SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premA_bycomp_rebal, 
                            AwarePP = SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premApp_bycomp_rebal,
                            A = test[[pop_name]]$A)
  
   if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 1){
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$\\tilde{\\mu}^*_{j,pb}(x)$")
      the_x_label <- NULL
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) == 4){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$\\tilde{\\mu}^*_{j,pb}(x)$")
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$\\tilde{\\mu}_{pb}(x|A=j)$")
    } else if(which(pop_name == names(df_to_g_A1)) %in% c(5, 6)){ # If it's on the left, apply correct xlabels
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- latex2exp::TeX("$\\tilde{\\mu}_{pb}(x|A=j)$")
    } else { 
      the_y_label <- NULL
      the_x_label <- NULL
    }
  
  ggplot(df_to_graph, aes(x=Aware, y=AwarePP, col=factor(A), shape=factor(A)))+
    geom_point(alpha=0.7)+
labs(x = the_x_label,
       y = the_y_label,
       title = latex2exp::TeX(long_labels_for_parms[pop_name]))+
    theme_classic()+
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5))+
   scale_color_manual(values=colors_for_gg_A, name="A")+
    scale_shape_manual(values=c(16,17),name="A")+
    geom_abline()
})%>% ggpubr::ggarrange(plotlist = .,
                           nrow = 1, ncol=3,
                           widths = 15, heights = 1,
                           common.legend = T,
                           legend = 'right'))


ggsave(filename = "figs/graph_rebal.pdf",
       plot = to_save_rebal,
       height = 3,
       width = 9,
       units = "in",
       device = "pdf", dpi = 500)
```

A table instead. 

```{r}
corr_table <-  names(df_to_g_A1)[1:3] %>% sapply(function(pop_name){
   
   c('Pearson' =   cor(SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premA_bycomp_rebal,
       SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premApp_bycomp_rebal, method = 'pearson'),
     'Kendall' = cor(SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premA_bycomp_rebal,
       SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premApp_bycomp_rebal, method = 'kendall'),
     'Spearman' = cor(SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premA_bycomp_rebal,
       SA_rebal_ratios[[pop_name]]$test_premApp_bycomp_rebal, method = 'spearman'))

})

xtable::xtable(corr_table, digits = 4)
```
